_comment_purpose: STEP 4 of Exercise 2 — Personal MCP example.
This file is an EXAMPLE of what a personal/experimental MCP server
block looks like. It belongs in YOUR ~/.claude.json, NOT in this
repository. We include it here only as a reference.
WHY two locations?
Project-level (.mcp.json, checked into git):
- Servers everyone needs
- Stable, reviewed by the team
- Updates ship via PRs
Personal (~/.claude.json, in your home dir):
- Experimental servers you're trying out
- Personal API keys you don't want anyone else using
- Tools relevant only to your workflow (e.g. your music app)
If you put experimental stuff in .mcp.json, you'll either commit
your secrets or every teammate will get errors about a server they
don't have credentials for. Keep them separate.
To use this example: copy the mcpServers block into your own
~/.claude.json (creating that file if needed). Anything in
~/.claude.json is merged with the project-level .mcp.json at
Claude Code startup — your personal servers appear alongside the
team ones.

experimental-jira._description: Personal Jira sandbox. Lets me query my assigned tickets without leaving Claude Code. Not shared with the team — they have their own credentials.
